title: APS Policy Officer
companySize: A division of 30-80 people inside a larger department
description: APS policy officers sit in the machinery of government. They research, draft, consult, clear briefs, revise advice, and help translate political direction into workable policy and implementation steps.

schedule.activity: Scan overnight developments and check ministerial or executive requests
Team check-in on priorities, deadlines, and clearance risks
Draft or revise a brief for the branch head or minister's office
Consult colleagues in legal, program, or finance about delivery implications
Working lunch while reviewing stakeholder submissions
Meeting with another department or state counterpart on implementation dependencies
Update the options paper and risk section after feedback
Send revised advice up the line and prepare tomorrow's briefing notes

responsibilities: Draft policy briefs, options papers, and speaking notes
Coordinate consultation and capture stakeholder feedback accurately
Work across legal, financial, and implementation constraints
Translate political priorities into administratively feasible options
challenges: Balancing evidence quality against political timing
Managing multiple clearance rounds and conflicting views
Working under ministerial pressure without losing analytical discipline
rewards: You learn how government really works
The work builds judgment and writing quickly
Good policy work can influence major public outcomes
